highlights.title: This humanoid robotics company is going public, but its CEO isn’t promising a robot in your home anytime soon
highlights.description: While other humanoid startups chase sky-high valuations, Agility Robotics is betting its future on execution — and a SPAC.

highlights.title: ByteDance, Alibaba Pull AI Companions as Beijing Tightens Rules
highlights.description: ByteDance Ltd. and Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. are pulling the plug on features that let users build and chat with AI companions, preparing for new Chinese regulations governing human interactions with artificial intelligence.

highlights.title: Apple veteran's Chinese smart-glasses firm becomes unicorn as Tencent, Meituan fund rival to Meta
highlights.description: The Shenzhen-based startup joined a slew of global peers to build advanced gadgets and electronics that bring the benefits of AI to individual users.

highlights.title: Nvidia Supplier Unimicron Seeks $1.4 Billion in GDS Offer
highlights.description: Unimicron Technology Corp. is seeking as much as $1.4 billion from a sale of global depositary shares, according to terms of the deal seen by Bloomberg News, capitalizing on an artificial-intelligence frenzy that has lifted the Taiwanese chip supplier’s stock more than 700% over the past 12 months.

highlights.title: Coinbase AI draws backlash after erroneously publishing World Cup result before kickoff
highlights.description: CEO Brian Armstrong investigated, and the firm said it made updates to prevent future AI-generated inaccuracies.

highlights.title: Meta’s woes deepen in India as child abuse ads on Instagram draw government ire
highlights.description: U.S. social media giant Meta is facing an onslaught of regulatory scrutiny in India, which has the largest user base for Facebook, Instagram and WhatsApp.

highlights.title: South Korea's SK Hynix, Samsung to Put AI Trade to the Test | The Pulse 7/6/2026
highlights.description:
South Korea’s memory giants will put the artificial-intelligence trade to yet another test this week. Samsung releases earnings on Tuesday. Investors are also getting ready for SK Hynix’s massive US listing days later.

Eleven people were killed and 60 injured in a deadly overnight attack in the Ukrainian capital, Kyiv, on the eve of the NATO summit in Ankara where President Donald Trump is due to meet with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.

Shares in UK budget carrier EasyJet soared after it agreed in principle to a takeover offer of more than £5 billion from US firm Castlelake. The bid was Castlelake’s fifth, after the two sides sparred over several weeks.

Today's guests: Monica Defend, Amundi Investment Institute Head; Elisabeth Braw, Atlantic Council Senior Fellow (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: Self-Driving Startup Turing Gets AMD Backing, Adopts AMD GPUs
highlights.description: Self-driving tech developer Turing Inc. has added AMD Ventures to its list of backers and begun adopting Advanced Micro Devices Inc.’s AI accelerators in its systems.

highlights.title: Wisconsin residents file class-action lawsuit against Microsoft's 'world's most powerful AI data center' due to data center noise — plaintiffs also mention construction noise and extreme light pollution from $7.3 billion facility
highlights.description: Wisconsin residents file class-action lawsuit against Microsoft due to data center noise — plaintiffs also mention construction noise and extreme light pollution

highlights.title: Singapore Files New Charges in Nvidia Chip Fraud Case
highlights.description: Singaporean prosecutors filed additional charges including money laundering against a key suspect in an AI server fraud case, part of the Southeast Asian nation’s growing efforts to clamp down on Nvidia Corp. chips being diverted illicitly to other locations including China.

robotics.title: This humanoid robotics company is going public, but its CEO isn’t promising a robot in your home anytime soon
robotics.description: While other humanoid startups chase sky-high valuations, Agility Robotics is betting its future on execution — and a SPAC.

ai.title: Apple Supplier Luxshare Said to Plan Pricing HK Listing at Top
ai.description: Apple Inc. supplier Luxshare Precision Industry Co. is poised to raise HK$24.3 billion ($3.1 billion) after telling prospective investors it wants to price shares for its Hong Kong listing at the maximum amount it had set, according to people familiar with the matter.

ai.title: Nvidia's next-gen AI rack system delayed to 2028 on manufacturing snags, SemiAnalysis says
ai.description: The reported delay adds to concerns that Nvidia's breakneck annual release cadence is colliding with manufacturing limits.

ai.title: Nvidia AI Server Delay Report Sends Asian Tech Stocks Sliding
ai.description: Asian technology stocks slumped after a report that Nvidia Corp.’s next-generation AI server rack system has been delayed by more than a year due to manufacturing difficulties.

ai.title: ITV to Sell Broadcasting Unit to Sky for Up to £1.6 Billion
ai.description: ITV Plc has agreed to sell its media and entertainment arm to Comcast Corp.-owned Sky Group Ltd. in a deal worth as much as £1.6 billion ($2.1 billion) including debt, a move that will reshape the UK television industry.

Eleven people were killed and 60 injured in a deadly overnight attack in the Ukrainian capital, Kyiv, on the eve of the NATO summit in Ankara where President Donald Trump is due to meet with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.

business.title: Smart glasses maker Even Realities hits $1B valuation with $150M funding led by Meituan, Tencent
business.description: Even Realities, an ex-Apple team building camera-free smart glasses, raised $150M from Meituan and Tencent at a $1B valuation.

business.title: Rakuten Travel Xchange CEO on Japan's Tourism
business.description:
James Park, CEO at Rakuten Travel Xchange, discusses the trends shaping Japan’s tourism market as inbound arrivals extended its decline in May, weighed down by reduced flights across routes and a prolonged slump in Chinese visitors. He speaks with Shery Ahn on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade."
 (Source: Bloomberg)

space.title: Apple's foldable iPhone may be in short supply after it launches
space.description: Many people who want Apple's rumored folding iPhone 'Ultra' may not be able to get it at first

space.title: FOMC minutes, SpaceX joins Nasdaq 100: Crypto Week Ahead
space.description: Your look at what's coming in the week starting July 6.

regulation.title: Memory Chipmaker SK Hynix Kicks Off $28 Billion US Listing
regulation.description: SK Hynix Inc. kicked off its formal marketing process for its US listing on Monday, as the South Korean chipmaker looks to capitalize on surging investor demand for the high-flying memory-chip sector.

regulation.title: Vitalik Buterin says Ethereum is preparing its 'biggest rebuild' since the Merge
regulation.description: A revised roadmap details how the multi-year overhaul would replace nearly every major part of Ethereum's protocol, with quantum resistance and privacy moving up the priority list. It lands as ether rallies more than 12% in the past seven days.
